description: Phase-0 golden fixtures for the precision-message graph operator (Docs/graph_precision_message_framework.md, Section 21, amended 2026-07-18). Each case is a tiny Gaussian system built from PAIRWISE relation factors p*(z_receiver - beta*z_informer)^2 plus optional clamps (hard-known lit nodes), finite-precision observations, and innovation anchors. Expected means/variances are exact closed-form numbers agreed BEFORE the operator is implemented; tests/test_graph_message_golden.py checks them against an independent brute-force dense Gaussian reference. Phases 1-2 must reproduce them through graph/message.py + graph/message_posterior.py.

conventions.factor: one entry = one relation factor precision*(z[receiver] - beta*z[informer])^2; canonical orientation per spec 7.6 (calendar: receiver = shorter maturity)
conventions.q: receiver conditional precision at a node = sum over incident factors of (precision if node is the receiver, precision*beta^2 if node is the informer) per the spec 7.6 unit mapping
conventions.units_invariance: scaling ALL precisions (factors + observations + anchors) by c leaves every posterior mean unchanged and divides every variance by c

cases.name: calendar_full_transmission
cases.note: alphaT=1 shape betas T_informer/T_receiver in canonical short-receiver orientation; z(6M)=+1 propagates at FULL amplitude: +2.0 to 3M, +0.5 to 1Y. C1Y's q is p*beta^2=16 because the 6M-1Y factor's canonical receiver is 6M (spec 7.6 mapping).

cases.name: competing_equal
cases.note: equal-precision opposing beta-one signals cancel in the mean; conditional precisions ADD (q=2p=8), var=1/(2p).

cases.name: cross_asset_average
cases.note: two lit assets, equal precision, beta one: plain average, q=2p.

cases.name: beta_adjusted_competition
cases.note: signals average AFTER mapping into receiver units: (0.5*(-1)+2*(+1))/2 = +0.75 — equal raw innovations do NOT cancel when betas differ (alphaT=1 maps 3M and 1Y into 6M with betas 0.5 and 2).

cases.name: multi_hop_variance
cases.note: chain A->B->C: means multiply through betas (1.5, then 0.75 total); Var(C) = (b2 b1)^2 vA + b2^2/p1 + 1/p2 = 0.05625+0.0625+0.125. As the source observation precision -> inf, Var(C) -> b2^2/p1 + 1/p2 = 0.1875 (spec 21.6: high-precision-source limit recovers the pure edge accumulation).

cases.name: dead_informer
cases.note: D is dark with no lit path and only a one-way edge into C, configured 25x MORE precise than the lit edge. Pairwise factors give ZERO dilution: mean(C) = 1.5*0.8 = 1.2 exactly and var(C) = 1/p_lit (the D-factor marginalizes away); the joint stays proper (PD). The rejected row-normalized form is improper here, and under a jitter anchor dilutes mean(C) toward p_lit/(p_lit+p_dead)*1.2 ~ 0.046 (spec 7.4 rejection rationale).

cases.name: shrunk_transfer
cases.note: node-linked anchor (chosen 2026-07-18, backtest/results/message_phase0.json): kappa is a FIXED per-node constant calibrated from the primary relation class, kappa = p_primary*(1-rho)/rho. Single-source transfer is EXACTLY rho*beta*z (0.34*2*1 = 0.68) with var = rho/p; rho=1 gives kappa=0 and recovers full transmission (case 21.1 semantics).

cases.name: shrunk_transfer_corroboration
cases.note: node-linked corroboration contract: kappa stays FIXED at the single-source calibration (NOT rescaled as edges arrive), so a second equal agreeing source lifts the transfer from rho to 2rho/(1+rho) = 0.5075 per unit beta (mean 2*0.5075 = 1.0149). This is the empirically validated behaviour: stored-row study measured single-source index slope 0.391 -> predicted two-source 0.563 vs measured 0.561. The rejected edge-linked rule (kappa ~ sum of incoming precisions) would pin the transfer at rho regardless of corroboration, contradicting the data.

cases.name: repeated_path_no_double_count
cases.note: T hears the same uncertain source twice (direct A->T and via B). Global solve: Var(T) = 5/(3p) = 5/12. Naive independent-path precision addition would claim 6/(5p) = 0.3 — falsely tighter. The covariance-aware solve must give 5/12, NOT 0.3. Var(A) stays 1/r = 0.25: uninformed neighbours add nothing back.
